rulings.Case Title: JAMES MANN ET AL VS. SIDE, INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Plaintiffs James Mann & Lauren Dudley
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 9, 2026, line 1, DEFENDANTS SIDE, INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION D/B/A KINOKO REAL ESTATE, ADRIAN TAN Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Plaintiffs James Mann & Lauren Dudley Defendants Side, Inc. and Adrian Tan's unopposed motion to compel the depositions of James Mann and Lauren Dudley is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. The parties are ordered to meet and confer forthwith, and by no later than March 11, about dates. If Mann and Dudley fail to provide reasonable dates, defendants may set the dates. Defendants seek sanctions. The failure to submit to an authorized method of discovery is a misuse of the discovery process, and Plaintiffs' failure to provide dates or cooperate to submit to depositions lacks substantial justification. The court orders Plaintiffs, jointly and severally, to pay $2510 to Side Inc. and Tan by March 23, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ANA MARIA CUEVAS VS. INTERNATIONAL TRAINING & EXCHANGE, INC. D/B/A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Amended Motion To Dismiss Entire Paga Action Without Prejudice; Declaration Of Joseph C. Ramli; [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 9, 2026, line 4, PLAINTIFF ANA CUEVAS , ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AS AN AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEE, THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, AND OTHER AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES Notice Of Motion And Amended Motion To Dismiss Entire Paga Action Without Prejudice The unopposed motion to dismiss entire PAGA action without prejudice is continued for hearing to April 9, 2026. Plaintiff's submission gives the court insufficient information from which to determine that the settlement of this PAGA action, which the court is required to approve as to the disposition of the PAGA claims, is fair to absent aggrieved employees. By one week in advance of the continued hearing date, Plaintiff must file a supplemental brief, with supporting declarations if needed, that allows the court either to conclude that the amount of Plaintiff's settlement is reasonable and does not leverage the dismissed claims of absent aggrieved employees to Plaintiff's benefit, or that other factors supply good cause to approve the dismissal as fair to absent employees. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KAPOLEI ENERGY STORAGE I, LLC VS. MOSS & ASSOCIATES, LLC,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Electric Power Systems International, Inc. And Compel Attendance At Person Most Qualified Deposition; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support T
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 9, 2026, line 5, PLAINTIFF KAPOLEI ENERGY STORAGE I, LLC'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Electric Power Systems International, Inc. And Compel Attendance At Person Most Qualified Deposition The unopposed motion by Kapolei Energy Storage I, LLC (KES) to compel production of documents and attendance at PMQ deposition by Electric Power Systems International, Inc. (EPS), is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. EPS shall produce documents in accord with its statements of compliance by March 23, 2026. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.320.) EPS shall meet and confer forthwith and by no later than March 16, 2026 about a date for the deposition of its PMQ and shall identify its PMQ. KES seeks sanctions. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2023.010, subd. (d); id., sec. 2023.020; id., sec. 2031.320, subd. (b).) KES's motion does not include a declaration "setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought" (id., sec. 2023.040), and KES does not request a particular amount. The court concludes that EPS's failure to submit to authorized methods of discovery is a misuse of the discovery process unsupported by substantial justification and orders EPS to pay $1000 in sanctions to KES within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SARKIS GEGAMYAN VS. ATLASSIAN US, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) And Special Interrogatories (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, March 9, 2026, line 6 PLAINTIFF SARKIS GEGAMYAN'S Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) And Special Interrogatories (Set One) By separate notice of motion filed on March 9, 2026, plaintiff Sarkis Gegamyan appears to have filed an additional motion to compel the same discovery that is at issue in the companion motion. It is unclear whether Gegamyan intended to file two motions or whether this additional filing should be construed as support for the motion filed on February 20th. To the extent it is a separate motion, the court orders it off calendar as untimely filed and served. To the extent it is support for the February 20th motion, the court has considered the additional filings in connection with that motion. Because defendant did not separate respond to this filing, no sanctions are ordered. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)
